headline: Apple’s China iPhone Sales Are Up. Thank Memory Cost Increases.
summary: Apple  iPhone sales outperformed competitors in China, new data show, signaling the company’s ability to persevere against a volatile industry backdrop.  For the first nine weeks of 2026, overall China smartphone sales declined 4% from the prior year, according to a report published by Counterpoint Research on Thursday.  This has caused a shortage in supply, but it’s been a tailwind for suppliers such as  Micron Technology  which reported second-quarter revenue growth of 196% on Wednesday night.

raw.headline: Apple’s China iPhone Sales Are Up. Thank Memory Cost Increases.
raw.summary: Apple  iPhone sales outperformed competitors in China, new data show, signaling the company’s ability to persevere against a volatile industry backdrop.  For the first nine weeks of 2026, overall China smartphone sales declined 4% from the prior year, according to a report published by Counterpoint Research on Thursday.  This has caused a shortage in supply, but it’s been a tailwind for suppliers such as  Micron Technology  which reported second-quarter revenue growth of 196% on Wednesday night.

headline: MU Q1 Deep Dive: AI Demand, Tight Supply, and Strategic Agreements Shape Outlook
summary: Memory chips maker Micron (NYSE:MU) reported revenue ahead of Wall Street’s expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 196% year on year to $23.86 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($33.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 41.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $12.20 per share was 38.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($33.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 41.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $12.20 per share was 38.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Micron shares slip as hefty spending plans eclipse strong AI-fueled earnings
summary: Micron's shares dropped more than 4% before the bell on Thursday, as the chipmaker's plans for heftier capex spending ‌unnerved investors, taking the shine off another round of AI-fueled blockbuster ‌quarterly earnings.  Micron, whose shares have climbed more than 61% this year after surging over 240% in 2025, ​said it is boosting its 2026 capital spending plan by $5 billion to meet growing demand, bringing its total investment for the current fiscal year to more than $25 billion.  "Investors wager that these are peak earnings and will be unsustainable," said Mike O'Rourke, chief ​market ​strategist at JonesTrading.

raw.headline: Micron shares slip as hefty spending plans eclipse strong AI-fueled earnings
raw.summary: Micron's shares dropped more than 4% before the bell on Thursday, as the chipmaker's plans for heftier capex spending ‌unnerved investors, taking the shine off another round of AI-fueled blockbuster ‌quarterly earnings.  Micron, whose shares have climbed more than 61% this year after surging over 240% in 2025, ​said it is boosting its 2026 capital spending plan by $5 billion to meet growing demand, bringing its total investment for the current fiscal year to more than $25 billion.  "Investors wager that these are peak earnings and will be unsustainable," said Mike O'Rourke, chief ​market ​strategist at JonesTrading.

headline: This Is How You Track Risk Of Your Micron Stock Investment
summary: Micron Technology (MU) reported its Q2 FY26 earnings yesterday, March 18, and the data was staggering. Non-GAAP EPS hit $12.20, a huge jump vs last quarter that crushed consensus estimates. How did this happen? The revenue skyrocketed 196% year-over-year while gross margins expanded massively to nearly 75%. And despite a very strong guidance, the stock dropped roughly 4.4% in after-hours trading. Why.
